message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objectives</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The primary objective was to evaluate the preliminary impact of
                    <jats:italic>Essential Coaching for Every Mother</jats:italic>
                    on self-efficacy, social support, postpartum anxiety and postpartum depression. The second objective was to explore the acceptability of the
                    <jats:italic>Essential Coaching for Every Mother</jats:italic>
                    program provided during the COVID-19 pandemic.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    A prospective pre-post study was conducted with first time mothers in Nova Scotia, Canada between July 15
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    and September 19
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    , 2020. Participants completed a self-report survey at enrolment (after birth) and six-weeks postpartum. Various standardized measures were used and qualitative feedback on the program was also collected. Paired t-tests were carried out to determine changes from baseline to follow-up on psychosocial outcomes and qualitative feedback was analysed through thematic analysis.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    A total of 88 women enrolled. Self-efficacy increased between baseline (B) and follow-up (F) (
                    <jats:italic>B</jats:italic>
                    :33.33;
                    <jats:italic>F</jats:italic>
                    :37.11,
                    <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                    =0.000) while anxiety (STAI) declined (
                    <jats:italic>B</jats:italic>
                    :38.49;
                    <jats:italic>F</jats:italic>
                    :34.79; p=0.004). In terms of acceptability, 89% of participants felt that the number of messages were just right, 84.5% felt the messages contained all the information they needed relative to caring for a newborn and 98.8% indicated they would recommend this program to other new mothers.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    <jats:italic>Essential Coaching for Every Mother</jats:italic>
                    may play a role in increasing maternal self-efficacy and decreasing anxiety, although future work with a control group is needed to delineate the true effects of the program. Overall, mothers were satisfied with the
                    <jats:italic>Essential Coaching for Every Mother</jats:italic>
                    program and would recommend it for other mothers, during COVID-19 and beyond.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Implementing Essential Coaching for Every Mother during COVID-19: A Pilot Pre-Post Intervention Study
